reasoning: Beyond Meat's primary threat to survival is its $1.1B zero-interest convertible note, but this debt does not mature until March 15, 2027. The company's cash runway was previously a major concern (causing the high community prediction), but on May 7, 2025, they announced raising an additional $100 million in debt financing from a non-profit plant-based advocate. Adding this to their reported $115.8 million cash on hand at the end of Q1 2025, their liquidity exceeds $215 million. With projected annual cash burn for 2025 estimated at less than $85 million, they have far more than enough runway to clear the end of 2025. Furthermore, multiple experts and analysts have stated that the 'base case' for a Chapter 11 filing is now 2027 when the notes mature. Since the resolution date requires bankruptcy before January 1, 2026, the short-term triggers have been effectively neutralized. The only remaining risks are sudden operational collapse or unexpected technical defaults, keeping the probability at a very low 6%.
